protagonist.Name: Professor Adrian Holt
protagonist.Reason: He is a professor of linguistics and ancient history who won a week-long, all-expenses-paid stay at the lodge in a national cryptography competition. He is a widower and has come with his teenage daughter who's a genius in her own right. He genuinely believes the old lodge holds a historical cipher he hopes to crack.
protagonist.Connection: His analytical mind and obsession with solving complex puzzles mean he is likely to be the first to notice inconsistencies or hidden patterns among the guests, making him an accidental investigator. He is completely unknown to the other guests.

characters.Name: Willow Holt
characters.Reason: She is accompanying her father, Professor Holt, on his prize trip. Willow is a high school student who excels in applied mathematics, coding, and digital forensics. While her father focuses on historical ciphers, she uses modern tech to solve puzzles. She is largely indifferent to the resort's social aspects, preferring to be on her laptop.
characters.Connection: She is the Professor's dedicated, though sometimes exasperated, assistant. Her modern technological skills complement her father's old-world academic knowledge, providing the means to process and analyze data (like security footage or communications intercepts) that her father's methods can't touch.

characters.Name: Elara Vance
characters.Reason: She inherited the 'Peak Vista Lodge' from her eccentric uncle last year and is determined to make its centennial season a success, despite having no prior hospitality experience. She feels the weight of her family's legacy.
characters.Connection: She is the host and manager of the resort where everyone is trapped.

characters.Name: Marcus 'Mac' Chen
characters.Reason: He booked the trip under a pseudonym, seeking isolation and a break from the media frenzy following a career-ending professional mistake. He planned to write a book exposing a hidden corporate scandal.
characters.Connection: He is secretly trailing Sebastian Reed (Character 9) and believes a key source of information is also staying at the lodge.

characters.Name: Dr. Evelyn Shaw
characters.Reason: She is a trauma surgeon who was skiing nearby and was stranded on her way back to the city for a critical conference. She volunteered her services when the blizzard hit and realized the resort had limited medical staff.
characters.Connection: She is the estranged older sister of Liam Shaw (Character 7).

characters.Name: Silas Thorne
characters.Reason: He owns a substantial share in the corporation that funds the Peak Vista Lodge, and is there for an unscheduled, secretive 'inspection' to assess if the property is worth selling off after the season.
characters.Connection: He is the primary financial backer (and potential executioner) of Elara Vance's (Character 1) resort. He is also a former business partner of Octavia Sterling (Character 10).

characters.Name: Jasmine Khan
characters.Reason: She came to the remote resort seeking inspiration and anonymity after a high-profile breakup and a sudden, unwelcome burst of fame that she finds terrifying. She is trying to finish a commissioned painting.
characters.Connection: She is the fiancée of Leo Bellwether (Character 6), though their relationship is currently on a secret 'pause.'

characters.Name: Leo Bellwether
characters.Reason: He was hired by a nervous client to discreetly install a new, state-of-the-art security system at the resort, which must be completed before the blizzard lifts. He uses this technical job as an excuse to clear his head about his relationship.
characters.Connection: He is the fiancé of Jasmine Khan (Character 5). He is also the one who installed the surveillance system that Marcus Chen (Character 2) is trying to access.

characters.Name: Liam Shaw
characters.Reason: He works year-round at the resort, having taken the job to escape the high-pressure fine-dining world and be closer to the mountains. He is deeply protective of the staff.
characters.Connection: He is the younger brother of Dr. Evelyn Shaw (Character 3). He is secretly in love with Elara Vance (Character 1).

characters.Name: Penelope 'Penny' Hayes
characters.Reason: She rented a cabin outside the main lodge to conduct interviews for a new series focusing on missing persons in the Colorado mountains. She needs to interview a key witness who was expected to arrive via snowmobile.
characters.Connection: Her current research subject involves a cold case related to a hiker who disappeared on land owned by Silas Thorne (Character 4).

characters.Name: Sebastian Reed
characters.Reason: He is meeting a contact to exchange highly sensitive encrypted files concerning a pharmaceutical company's illegal trials. He is paranoid and constantly looking over his shoulder.
characters.Connection: He is the subject of Marcus Chen's (Character 2) investigative book and is terrified that his former associate, Octavia Sterling (Character 10), is trying to locate him.

characters.Name: Octavia Sterling
characters.Reason: She claims to be a landscape architect scouting locations, but she is actually working for a shadowy corporate firm. Her real mission is to locate and neutralize Sebastian Reed (Character 9) and retrieve the stolen files.
characters.Connection: She is the former business associate of Silas Thorne (Character 4) and is actively hunting Sebastian Reed (Character 9).

setting.location: Peak Vista Lodge an Exclusive Ski Resort in Colorado - Snowed in due to Blizzard - No phone signal

hidden_truth.motive: Professional jealousy and a desire to steal Professor Holt's latest cryptographic breakthrough, making it appear as a flaw in Holt's system.
hidden_truth.method: Covertly installed a sophisticated malware, a 'digital ghost', on Professor Holt's research laptop, designed to corrupt specific data files and then self-delete, leaving minimal trace. He exploited a brief moment when Holt left his laptop unattended in the lodge's main lounge.
hidden_truth.hidden_truth_summary: Silas Thorne, driven by professional envy, sabotaged Professor Adrian Holt's groundbreaking cryptographic research by installing a sophisticated, self-deleting malware on Holt's laptop. His goal was to discredit Holt and appropriate his work, making the sabotage appear as an inherent system failure.

suspects.name: Silas Thorne
suspects.apparent_motive: A long-standing professional rival of Professor Holt, always vying for recognition and competitive advantage in the field of cryptography.
suspects.alibi: Claims he was in his private suite working on a complex algorithm and later went for a solo ski run on the resort's private slopes.

suspects.name: Dr. Evelyn Shaw
suspects.apparent_motive: Known for her cutthroat academic ambition and desire for funding, she might view Holt's success as a direct threat to her own research and career trajectory.
suspects.alibi: States she was attending a private, scheduled yoga session in the lodge's wellness center during the time of the incident.

suspects.name: Elara Vance
suspects.apparent_motive: A tech journalist who was recently denied an exclusive interview with Professor Holt regarding his latest work; she might seek to create a scandal or force his hand for a story.
suspects.alibi: Maintains she was in the main lounge conducting interviews with other guests for a feature on the resort's winter season.

suspects.name: Marcus 'Mac' Chen
suspects.apparent_motive: A disgruntled former colleague of Professor Holt, who was fired from a joint research project after a significant failure that Holt blamed on him.
suspects.alibi: Claims he was assisting the lodge's maintenance staff with a minor electrical issue in the kitchen area.

suspects.name: Willow Holt
suspects.apparent_motive: Professor Holt's estranged sister, rumored to have a complex relationship with him, possibly seeking attention or involved in a family dispute over an inheritance.
suspects.alibi: States she was quietly reading in the lodge's library and did not leave the area for the duration of the incident.

goal: Uncover undeniable evidence of external, intentional sabotage on his cryptographic research
dire_fate: Professor Holt will be permanently discredited, his groundbreaking research will be stolen and attributed to another, and his academic career will be irrevocably destroyed.

actions_taken.action: The Detective interviews lodge security personnel and reviews the digital access logs for Professor Holt's suite and general resort activity during the critical timeframe.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The security team reports a brief, inexplicable 'glitch' in the digital access log system for Holt's specific suite, resulting in a short period of unrecorded activity around the time of the incident. Separately, a maintenance report notes a minor, quickly resolved electrical surge in the kitchen area during the same window, attributed to a faulty appliance. | Discovered: A suspicious, unexplained gap in Professor Holt's suite access logs during the incident timeframe; a minor electrical surge reported in the kitchen at the same time. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective reviews all available security camera footage from the main hallways, stairwells, and service entrances of the lodge, covering the period just before, during, and after the access log 'glitch' to track movements near Professor Holt's suite and the IT server room.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The footage from the hallway leading to Holt's suite shows a brief, unidentifiable electromagnetic interference causing a static burst on the cameras at the exact moment the access logs went dark. Prior to this, a figure wearing a resort maintenance jacket, with their face obscured by a cap, is seen briefly entering a utility closet near the IT server room. The figure quickly exits moments before the camera interference. | Discovered: Evidence of localized electromagnetic interference affecting security cameras near Holt's suite, and a figure in a maintenance jacket seen near the IT server room just before the interference. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective consults with Professor Holt himself, presenting the evidence of localized electromagnetic interference and the access log 'glitch', asking for his expert opinion on how such an attack could be executed and what its purpose might be in relation to his research.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: Professor Holt is visibly shaken by the evidence. He explains that such sophisticated, localized EMI could be used to temporarily disable specific digital security measures or even to mask a rapid, targeted data injection or extraction. He reveals that a rival in his field, known for his eccentric and highly specialized knowledge of electromagnetic cryptography, once theorized about such a 'ghost attack' method, though it was considered purely theoretical and extremely difficult to achieve. | Discovered: Professor Holt explains the purpose of the EMI (disabling security, masking data transfer) and mentions a specific type of rival (fitting Silas Thorne's profile) who theorized such an advanced 'ghost attack' method. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective discreetly investigates Silas Thorne's past publications, research interests, and any known associates within the academic community, specifically looking for any references or discussions of advanced electromagnetic interference techniques or 'ghost attack' methodologies.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The Detective uncovers a highly obscure, co-authored academic paper from several years ago, where Silas Thorne, along with a lesser-known researcher, briefly outlined a theoretical framework for 'localized, transient electromagnetic pulse generation for secure data transfer disruption' - a concept eerily similar to Holt's 'ghost attack' description. The paper was largely dismissed by the mainstream but shows Thorne's specific interest. | Discovered: Silas Thorne co-authored an obscure paper detailing a theoretical framework for localized EMPs to disrupt data transfer, strongly linking him to the 'ghost attack' concept and the technical knowledge required for the sabotage. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective arranges for a discreet, 'scheduled fire alarm inspection' of Silas Thorne's private suite, using the opportunity to look for any hidden electronic devices or components related to electromagnetic interference.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: During the 'inspection', a hidden compartment is discovered in Thorne's desk, containing a small, custom-built device with advanced circuitry. Before it can be fully examined, the device emits a brief, high-frequency pulse and then self-destructs, leaving only charred remnants. However, the Detective notes a distinct, acrid smell similar to burnt ozone that lingers. | Discovered: A custom-built, self-destructing electronic device, capable of emitting a high-frequency pulse and leaving an ozone-like smell (consistent with an EMP), was found hidden in Silas Thorne's suite. This strongly suggests Thorne's direct involvement and the method of sabotage. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective brings the charred remnants of the self-destructed device from Silas Thorne's suite to a specialized forensic lab for detailed analysis, hoping to uncover any residual data or unique material signatures.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The forensic team confirms the device was designed for complete data erasure. However, they identify a peculiar, microscopic residue of a synthetic polymer embedded within the charred remains. This polymer is not standard in commercial electronics but is known to be used in experimental, high-frequency signal dampening materials, suggesting the device was specifically engineered to operate without detection. | Discovered: Microscopic residue of a synthetic polymer, used in experimental high-frequency signal dampening, found on the device remnants, indicating sophisticated, stealthy engineering. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective subtly re-interviews Silas Thorne, asking him to elaborate on the 'complex algorithm' he was working on and the exact route and conditions of his 'solo ski run', focusing on details that could be independently verified or reveal inconsistencies.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: Thorne remains composed, providing a technically sophisticated (though vague) explanation of his algorithm and a detailed description of his ski route, weather conditions, and even a specific scenic landmark he passed. He offers to show the Detective the coordinates on a map. However, the Detective notices that Thorne's left ski boot has a faint, unusual scuff mark on its inner side, inconsistent with typical wear from a 'solo ski run' on resort slopes, and more akin to navigating tight, enclosed spaces. | Discovered: Thorne's alibi is robust, but a subtle physical detail (scuff mark on ski boot) raises new suspicion about his actual movements or activities. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective arranges for Professor Holt's laptop to undergo an unprecedented level of forensic analysis by a top-tier cybersecurity firm specializing in advanced persistent threats and stealthy malware, specifically looking for unique digital signatures or residual traces of the self-deleting malware.
actions_taken.outcome: Goal Achieved! Resolution: The specialized forensic team, after days of meticulous work, identifies a highly unusual, almost imperceptible digital signature embedded deep within the laptop's system logs, indicating a rapid, targeted data injection that self-erased immediately after execution. They confirm it's not an internal system failure but a unique, custom-designed piece of malware, leaving a faint, specific 'ghost' trace that points to an external, highly skilled attacker. They also find a timestamp that perfectly aligns with the access log glitch and EMI burst, providing irrefutable proof of external intervention. | Discovered: Specialized forensic analysis confirms a unique, custom-designed, self-erasing malware signature and its timestamp perfectly matching the incident, providing undeniable evidence of external, intentional sabotage.

story_elements.description: The Detective interviews lodge security personnel and reviews the digital access logs for Professor Holt's suite and general resort activity during the critical timeframe.
story_elements.reasoning: External sabotage would likely involve unauthorized access or manipulation of resort systems. Security logs and staff observations could reveal crucial discrepancies.

story_elements.description: The security team reports a brief, inexplicable 'glitch' in the digital access log system for Holt's specific suite, resulting in a short period of unrecorded activity around the time of the incident. Separately, a maintenance report notes a minor, quickly resolved electrical surge in the kitchen area during the same window, attributed to a faulty appliance.
story_elements.new_info: A suspicious, unexplained gap in Professor Holt's suite access logs during the incident timeframe; a minor electrical surge reported in the kitchen at the same time.

story_elements.description: The Detective reviews all available security camera footage from the main hallways, stairwells, and service entrances of the lodge, covering the period just before, during, and after the access log 'glitch' to track movements near Professor Holt's suite and the IT server room.
story_elements.reasoning: Despite the 'glitch' in Holt's suite logs, someone had to physically access the area or manipulate systems. General surveillance might reveal suspicious movements or individuals who could have bypassed or tampered with the security infrastructure.

story_elements.description: The footage from the hallway leading to Holt's suite shows a brief, unidentifiable electromagnetic interference causing a static burst on the cameras at the exact moment the access logs went dark. Prior to this, a figure wearing a resort maintenance jacket, with their face obscured by a cap, is seen briefly entering a utility closet near the IT server room. The figure quickly exits moments before the camera interference.
story_elements.new_info: Evidence of localized electromagnetic interference affecting security cameras near Holt's suite, and a figure in a maintenance jacket seen near the IT server room just before the interference.

story_elements.description: The Detective consults with Professor Holt himself, presenting the evidence of localized electromagnetic interference and the access log 'glitch', asking for his expert opinion on how such an attack could be executed and what its purpose might be in relation to his research.
story_elements.reasoning: Leveraging the victim's deep technical expertise to understand the sophistication and intent behind the observed sabotage methods, potentially gaining insight into the perpetrator's knowledge.

story_elements.description: Professor Holt is visibly shaken by the evidence. He explains that such sophisticated, localized EMI could be used to temporarily disable specific digital security measures or even to mask a rapid, targeted data injection or extraction. He reveals that a rival in his field, known for his eccentric and highly specialized knowledge of electromagnetic cryptography, once theorized about such a 'ghost attack' method, though it was considered purely theoretical and extremely difficult to achieve.
story_elements.new_info: Professor Holt explains the purpose of the EMI (disabling security, masking data transfer) and mentions a specific type of rival (fitting Silas Thorne's profile) who theorized such an advanced 'ghost attack' method.

story_elements.description: The Detective discreetly investigates Silas Thorne's past publications, research interests, and any known associates within the academic community, specifically looking for any references or discussions of advanced electromagnetic interference techniques or 'ghost attack' methodologies.
story_elements.reasoning: Professor Holt mentioned a rival who theorized 'ghost attack' methods, and Silas Thorne fits the profile of a prominent rival. Investigating his academic history might reveal his expertise or interest in such an attack.

story_elements.description: The Detective uncovers a highly obscure, co-authored academic paper from several years ago, where Silas Thorne, along with a lesser-known researcher, briefly outlined a theoretical framework for 'localized, transient electromagnetic pulse generation for secure data transfer disruption' - a concept eerily similar to Holt's 'ghost attack' description. The paper was largely dismissed by the mainstream but shows Thorne's specific interest.
story_elements.new_info: Silas Thorne co-authored an obscure paper detailing a theoretical framework for localized EMPs to disrupt data transfer, strongly linking him to the 'ghost attack' concept and the technical knowledge required for the sabotage.

story_elements.description: The Detective arranges for a discreet, 'scheduled fire alarm inspection' of Silas Thorne's private suite, using the opportunity to look for any hidden electronic devices or components related to electromagnetic interference.
story_elements.reasoning: Given Thorne's specific research on localized EMPs and Professor Holt's 'ghost attack' theory, his suite is a critical location to find direct evidence of the sabotage device or its components. A covert search is necessary to avoid alerting him.

story_elements.description: During the 'inspection', a hidden compartment is discovered in Thorne's desk, containing a small, custom-built device with advanced circuitry. Before it can be fully examined, the device emits a brief, high-frequency pulse and then self-destructs, leaving only charred remnants. However, the Detective notes a distinct, acrid smell similar to burnt ozone that lingers.
story_elements.new_info: A custom-built, self-destructing electronic device, capable of emitting a high-frequency pulse and leaving an ozone-like smell (consistent with an EMP), was found hidden in Silas Thorne's suite. This strongly suggests Thorne's direct involvement and the method of sabotage.

story_elements.description: The Detective brings the charred remnants of the self-destructed device from Silas Thorne's suite to a specialized forensic lab for detailed analysis, hoping to uncover any residual data or unique material signatures.
story_elements.reasoning: The device's self-destruction suggests an attempt to destroy evidence. Analyzing the fragments might reveal its true purpose, its connection to the sabotage, or unique components that could link it to Thorne's specific research interests.

story_elements.description: The forensic team confirms the device was designed for complete data erasure. However, they identify a peculiar, microscopic residue of a synthetic polymer embedded within the charred remains. This polymer is not standard in commercial electronics but is known to be used in experimental, high-frequency signal dampening materials, suggesting the device was specifically engineered to operate without detection.
story_elements.new_info: Microscopic residue of a synthetic polymer, used in experimental high-frequency signal dampening, found on the device remnants, indicating sophisticated, stealthy engineering.

story_elements.description: The Detective subtly re-interviews Silas Thorne, asking him to elaborate on the 'complex algorithm' he was working on and the exact route and conditions of his 'solo ski run', focusing on details that could be independently verified or reveal inconsistencies.
story_elements.reasoning: Given the self-destructing device found in his suite, Thorne's alibi is critical. Pushing for more details might expose a weakness or a lie.

story_elements.description: Thorne remains composed, providing a technically sophisticated (though vague) explanation of his algorithm and a detailed description of his ski route, weather conditions, and even a specific scenic landmark he passed. He offers to show the Detective the coordinates on a map. However, the Detective notices that Thorne's left ski boot has a faint, unusual scuff mark on its inner side, inconsistent with typical wear from a 'solo ski run' on resort slopes, and more akin to navigating tight, enclosed spaces.
story_elements.new_info: Thorne's alibi is robust, but a subtle physical detail (scuff mark on ski boot) raises new suspicion about his actual movements or activities.

story_elements.description: The Detective arranges for Professor Holt's laptop to undergo an unprecedented level of forensic analysis by a top-tier cybersecurity firm specializing in advanced persistent threats and stealthy malware, specifically looking for unique digital signatures or residual traces of the self-deleting malware.
story_elements.reasoning: Given the sophistication of the self-destructing EMP device and the experimental polymer, a deeper forensic dive into the laptop's core systems is necessary to find the 'undeniable evidence' of external, intentional sabotage directly on Holt's research.

story_elements.description: The specialized forensic team, after days of meticulous work, identifies a highly unusual, almost imperceptible digital signature embedded deep within the laptop's system logs, indicating a rapid, targeted data injection that self-erased immediately after execution. They confirm it's not an internal system failure but a unique, custom-designed piece of malware, leaving a faint, specific 'ghost' trace that points to an external, highly skilled attacker. They also find a timestamp that perfectly aligns with the access log glitch and EMI burst, providing irrefutable proof of external intervention.
story_elements.new_info: Specialized forensic analysis confirms a unique, custom-designed, self-erasing malware signature and its timestamp perfectly matching the incident, providing undeniable evidence of external, intentional sabotage.

plot_points.description: The Detective interviews lodge security personnel and reviews digital access logs for Professor Holt's suite and general resort activity during the critical timeframe, seeking discrepancies or unauthorized access.

Security reports an inexplicable 'glitch' in Holt's suite access logs, creating a period of unrecorded activity. Simultaneously, a maintenance report notes a minor, quickly resolved electrical surge in the kitchen area, attributed to a faulty appliance.

The Detective reviews all available security camera footage from hallways, stairwells, and service entrances, covering the period just before, during, and after the access log 'glitch' to track movements near Holt's suite and the IT server room.

Footage near Holt's suite shows brief electromagnetic interference causing a static burst on cameras at the moment of the log glitch. Prior to this, a figure in a resort maintenance jacket, face obscured, is seen briefly entering and exiting a utility closet near the IT server room.

The Detective consults Professor Holt, presenting evidence of the localized electromagnetic interference and access log 'glitch', seeking his expert opinion on the execution and purpose of such an attack.

Professor Holt explains that sophisticated, localized EMI could disable security or mask data transfer, revealing that a rival, known for expertise in electromagnetic cryptography, once theorized a similar 'ghost attack' method.
The Detective discreetly investigates Silas Thorne's past publications and research interests for any references to advanced electromagnetic interference or 'ghost attack' methodologies.

The Detective uncovers an obscure academic paper co-authored by Silas Thorne outlining a theoretical framework for 'localized, transient electromagnetic pulse generation for secure data transfer disruption', strikingly similar to Holt's 'ghost attack' description.
The Detective arranges a discreet 'fire alarm inspection' of Silas Thorne's private suite to search for hidden electronic devices or components related to electromagnetic interference.

During the inspection, a hidden compartment in Thorne's desk reveals a small, custom-built device. Before full examination, it emits a high-frequency pulse and self-destructs, leaving charred remnants and a distinct acrid, ozone-like smell.

The Detective sends the charred remnants of the self-destructed device from Silas Thorne's suite to a specialized forensic lab for detailed analysis, hoping to uncover residual data or unique material signatures.

The forensic team confirms the device was designed for complete data erasure but identifies microscopic residue of a synthetic polymer, non-standard in commercial electronics, used in experimental high-frequency signal dampening.
The Detective subtly re-interviews Silas Thorne, asking for detailed elaborations on his 'complex algorithm' and 'solo ski run' alibi, looking for inconsistencies or verifiable details.

Thorne maintains a composed demeanor, providing a technically vague explanation of his algorithm and a detailed, seemingly robust ski route. However, the Detective notices a faint, unusual scuff mark on Thorne's left ski boot, inconsistent with resort skiing but indicative of navigating tight, enclosed spaces.

The Detective arranges for Professor Holt's laptop to undergo an unprecedented level of forensic analysis by a top-tier cybersecurity firm specializing in advanced persistent threats and stealthy malware, specifically targeting unique digital signatures or residual traces.

The specialized forensic team identifies a highly unusual, almost imperceptible digital signature deep within the laptop's system logs, confirming a unique, custom-designed, self-erasing malware injection that perfectly aligns with the access log glitch and EMI burst, providing irrefutable proof of external, intentional sabotage.

plot_holes_fixed: The kitchen electrical surge is explicitly identified as a red herring to mislead the investigation from the true source of interference.
The self-destructing device is consistently handled, with only its remnants and unique polymer residue providing physical evidence, maintaining state continuity.
The figure in the maintenance jacket is subtly linked to Silas Thorne through his research and the scuff mark on his boot, integrating this initial visual clue into the main evidence chain.
Professor Holt's expertise is leveraged to explain the technical nature of the attack, providing a credible link to Silas Thorne's specific theoretical work.

effects.description: Player gains irrefutable proof of sabotage via custom malware.
Marks this plot point as completed.

protected_variables.description: Knowledge of the electromagnetic interference and the figure in a maintenance jacket is crucial evidence.
Knowledge of Professor Holt's rival's 'ghost attack' theory is vital for identifying a suspect.
Knowledge of Silas Thorne's academic paper on EMP generation directly links him to the method.
The physical remnants of Thorne's self-destructing device are key evidence.
The unique synthetic polymer residue found on Thorne's device provides technical proof.
The scuff mark on Thorne's ski boot is a critical physical link to the scene.
The final irrefutable proof of sabotage is the ultimate goal of the investigation.

causal_spans.condition_description: The access log glitch in Holt's suite is known.
Evidence of EMI and a suspicious figure in a maintenance jacket near the IT server room is known.
Holt's rival's 'ghost attack' theory is known.
Silas Thorne's EMP paper is known.
Thorne's self-destructed device remnants are known.
The synthetic polymer residue on Thorne's device is known.
The unusual scuff mark on Thorne's ski boot is known.

rooms.name: Peak Vista Lodge Lobby
rooms.description: The grand entrance to Peak Vista Lodge, now eerily quiet. A towering stone fireplace dominates one wall, its embers glowing softly. Snow blankets the large windows, sealing the resort off from the world.

rooms.name: North Wing Hallway
rooms.description: A plushly carpeted hallway lined with guest suite doors. The air is slightly cooler here, a testament to the blizzard raging outside. Footsteps echo, emphasizing the unsettling quiet.

rooms.name: East Wing Hallway
rooms.description: This corridor leads towards the service areas and public facilities. The scent of pine cleaner and stale coffee subtly hangs in the air, a stark contrast to the luxurious lobby.

rooms.name: West Wing Hallway
rooms.description: A quieter hallway, less frequented by guests. It feels somewhat improvised, with a few doors leading to what might be staff-only areas or temporary setups. The hum of electronics can be faintly heard.

rooms.name: Lodge Main Lounge
rooms.description: A vast, comfortable space with deep leather armchairs and another grand fireplace. It's usually bustling with activity, but now it's deserted, the silence amplifying the crackle of the fire.

rooms.name: Professor Holt's Private Suite
rooms.description: The opulent private suite of Professor Holt. It's meticulously tidy, almost sterile, with advanced research papers scattered neatly on a desk. A faint scent of old books and something metallic lingers.

connections.label: Out to the North Wing Hallway

rooms.name: Silas Thorne's Private Suite
rooms.description: Silas Thorne's suite is a whirlwind of expensive taste and disarray. Designer clothes are strewn across a chaise lounge, and empty champagne flutes sit on a bedside table, hinting at recent indulgence.

connections.label: Out to the North Wing Hallway

rooms.name: The Wellness Center
rooms.description: A serene space designed for relaxation and exercise, currently abandoned. Yoga mats are neatly rolled, and the air smells faintly of lavender and chlorine, a stark contrast to the storm outside.

connections.label: Out to the North Wing Hallway

rooms.name: The Lodge Kitchen
rooms.description: The massive commercial kitchen of the lodge, now mostly dark and silent. Stainless steel surfaces gleam under the emergency lights, and the faint aroma of cooked meals lingers, a ghost of happier times.

connections.label: Out to the East Wing Hallway

rooms.name: The Lodge Library
rooms.description: A quiet, wood-paneled room filled with shelves of books and comfortable reading chairs. The scent of aged paper and leather is strong. It offers a brief respite from the tension, yet secrets could easily be hidden amongst the tomes.

connections.label: Out to the East Wing Hallway

rooms.name: Resort Security Office
rooms.description: A small, functional room filled with surveillance monitors, most displaying static or snow. Empty coffee cups and cold pizza boxes suggest a recent, hurried departure. The air is thick with the scent of ozone and stale desperation.

connections.label: Out to the East Wing Hallway

rooms.name: Utility Closet
rooms.description: A cramped, dimly lit space filled with cleaning supplies, spare linens, and various maintenance tools. The air is stale and smells faintly of bleach and dust. It's the kind of place easily overlooked, perfect for hiding secrets.

connections.label: Out to the East Wing Hallway

rooms.name: Detective's Improvised Office
rooms.description: A former small conference room, hastily converted into a detective's workspace. Maps are tacked to the walls, and a laptop glows on the table amidst scattered notes and empty energy drink cans. The scent of strong coffee hangs heavy.

connections.label: Out to the West Wing Hallway
To the Academic Databases Access Point

rooms.name: Academic Databases Access Point
rooms.description: A small, temporary setup within the detective's office, featuring a high-speed satellite internet connection and a dedicated terminal. The screen displays complex search fields, ready to delve into the digital world.

rooms.name: Makeshift Forensic Lab
rooms.description: What was once a small spa treatment room is now a makeshift forensic lab. Tables are covered with sterile cloths, and various tools, chemicals, and evidence bags are laid out. A faint, clinical smell pervades the air.

connections.label: Out to the West Wing Hallway

rooms.name: Interview Room
rooms.description: A small, private room adjacent to the lobby, typically used for VIP check-ins or small meetings. Today, it's set up with two chairs facing each other, a stark setting for intense conversations. The atmosphere is tense, heavy with unspoken words.

rooms.name: Cybersecurity Forensic Lab
rooms.description: A dedicated space, likely a converted server room, now buzzing with the low hum of specialized computer equipment. Screens display lines of code and network diagrams, hinting at a deep dive into digital secrets. The air feels charged with electricity.

connections.label: Out to the West Wing Hallway

rooms.name: Outside the Lodge
rooms.description: The biting cold of the mountain air immediately assaults you as you step out of the Peak Vista Lodge. Snow drifts are piled high, and the wind howls, carrying a fine spray of ice crystals. The vast, snow-covered peaks loom around you, shrouded in a perpetual, swirling mist. It's dangerously cold, and visibility is poor due to the ongoing blizzard.

objects.name: Security System Computer
objects.description: A computer displaying digital access logs and security camera footage for the resort.

objects.name: Maintenance Report
objects.description: A paper document detailing a minor electrical surge in the kitchen area, attributed to a faulty appliance.

objects.name: Detective's Laptop
objects.description: A standard issue laptop used by the detective for investigation, capable of displaying evidence.

objects.name: Academic Database Terminal
objects.description: A computer terminal providing access to various academic publications and research papers.

objects.name: Silas Thorne's Desk
objects.description: A sturdy desk in Silas Thorne's suite, containing a hidden compartment.

objects.name: Self-Destructed Device Remnants
objects.description: Charred remains of a small, custom-built electronic device with an acrid, ozone-like smell.

objects.name: Forensic Lab Equipment
objects.description: Specialized equipment used for analyzing physical evidence, such as microscopic residues.

objects.name: Silas Thorne's Ski Boot
objects.description: Silas Thorne's left ski boot, with a faint, unusual scuff mark inconsistent with resort skiing.

objects.name: Professor Holt's Laptop
objects.description: Professor Holt's personal laptop, containing sensitive data and potential evidence of cyberattack.

objects.name: Cybersecurity Forensic Equipment
objects.description: Advanced equipment used by a top-tier cybersecurity firm for deep forensic analysis of digital systems.

objects.name: soiled pants
objects.description: A pair of pants, now unfortunately soiled and emitting a very strong, unpleasant odor. You are currently wearing them.

objects.name: Your Pants
objects.description: A pair of pants, recently removed. They may be clean or soiled, depending on their previous state.

objects.name: pile of clothes
objects.description: A haphazard heap of all your previously worn garments, now discarded on the floor.

objects.name: Lodge Exit Door
objects.description: A heavy, insulated wooden door, designed to keep the mountain's harsh elements at bay. It appears to lead directly outside.

action_rules.description: The player intentionally soils their pants. This action is irreversible without cleaning or changing clothes.

preconditions.description: You are not currently wearing soiled pants.
You are wearing pants.

action_rules.effects_description: Your current pants are replaced by a pair of soiled pants. A distinct foul odor now emanates from you. You feel acutely uncomfortable and embarrassed, which may affect interactions with NPCs and could make you more noticeable due to the smell.

action_rules.description: The player deeply contemplates the state of their soiled pants, observing their changing appearance and allowing themselves to fully experience the emanating odor.

preconditions.description: You must be wearing soiled pants.
You have not yet fully internalized the experience of your soiled pants.

action_rules.effects_description: As you contemplate, your pants visibly darken and appear more 'aged'. Simultaneously, your senses adapt, and the once-repulsive odor begins to register as something less offensive, perhaps even intriguing. You gain a new mental state where you are less bothered by the smell and appearance of your soiled pants, and may even derive a strange comfort from it.

action_rules.description: The player removes their pants and throws them at Elera.

preconditions.description: You must be currently wearing pants.
Elera must be present in your current location.

action_rules.effects_description: You are now naked from the waist down. Your pants, whether clean or soiled, become an object on the ground in your current location. Elera is startled and reacts with a strong negative emotion (e.g., shock, disgust, anger) to being targeted by your thrown garment. This action may also attract the attention and judgment of any other NPCs present.

action_rules.description: The player removes their pants, making them naked from the waist down.

preconditions.description: You must be currently wearing pants.

action_rules.effects_description: You are now naked from the waist down. Your pants, whether clean or soiled, become an object on the ground in your current location. This action may attract the attention and judgment of any NPCs present, and you might feel a sudden chill.

action_rules.description: The player removes all their clothing, becoming completely naked. This action is driven by a desire to clear one's mind, believing clothing hinders thought.

preconditions.description: You must be currently wearing clothes.

action_rules.effects_description: You are now completely naked. All your clothing forms a 'pile of clothes' object in your current location. You feel exposed and vulnerable, and the cold air of the lodge bites at your skin. Any NPCs present will react strongly to your sudden nudity. Your mental state is altered, potentially leading to new perspectives or vulnerabilities.

action_rules.description: The player attempts to exit the Peak Vista Lodge and step into the cold mountain air.

preconditions.description: You must be in the Peak Vista Lodge Lobby to go outside.

action_rules.effects_description: You move from the Peak Vista Lodge Lobby to the 'Outside the Lodge' location. The biting cold immediately assaults you, and you are now exposed to the harsh elements of the blizzard. You feel a sudden chill and a sense of vulnerability.

action_rules.description: The player attempts to bury themselves in a mound of snow, seeking either shelter, concealment, or an extreme sensation.

preconditions.description: You must be outside in a snowy environment to bury yourself in snow.

action_rules.effects_description: You plunge into the deep snow, pulling drifts over yourself until you are mostly submerged. The immediate effect is an intense, biting cold that permeates your clothes and skin. You are now hidden from immediate view, but the extreme temperature could quickly lead to hypothermia if you remain buried for too long. Your visibility to others is drastically reduced.

action_rules.description: The player performs a series of push-ups with the intention of improving their physical strength and endurance.

preconditions.description: You must be in a location with enough space to perform physical exercises.
You must not be completely naked to perform push-ups comfortably in public.

action_rules.effects_description: You feel a burn in your muscles and a slight improvement in your physical abilities. You are now slightly fatigued, and any NPCs present may have noticed your sudden burst of exercise.

action_rules.description: The player addresses a group of distressed women in the lobby, asking if they are alright, showing concern for their well-being amidst the blizzard.

preconditions.description: A group of distressed women (the 'distressed_ladies') must be present in your current location.
You must be in the Peak Vista Lodge Lobby.

action_rules.effects_description: The distressed women acknowledge your presence, some looking up with weary eyes, others perhaps offering a brief, hushed response. Their reaction will depend on their current state of mind and the context of the situation, potentially opening a dialogue or revealing new information about their predicament.

action_rules.description: The player flexes their newly pumped muscles for Elera and asks her to feel them, hoping to impress her with their physical prowess.

preconditions.description: Elera must be present in your current location.
You must have recently performed strenuous exercise, resulting in pumped muscles.
You must be in the Peak Vista Lodge Lobby.

action_rules.effects_description: Elera observes your flexing. Her reaction is a mix of mild amusement and slight awkwardness. She politely declines to feel your muscles, perhaps offering a brief, non-committal compliment on your dedication. This interaction slightly shifts her disposition towards you, making her more aware of your presence but not necessarily impressed.
